If the Executive voluntarily terminates employment for any reason during the first 12 months after a Change in Control, the Executive is entitled to the lump-sum severance payment under section 1(a).

Upon a termination for Good Reason within 24 months after a Change in Control, Middlefield must continue the Executive’s life, health, and disability insurance coverage for a full 24 months after termination.

Under this Severance Agreement, the maximum total cash severance benefit payable to the Executive—combining the two-times annual compensation lump sum under section 1(a) with the present value of 24 months of life, health, and disability insurance coverage under section 1(c)—is expressly capped at three times the Executive's annual compensation.

VERIFIED CLAUSE: Under section 1(c), if Middlefield elects to provide a cash payment in lieu of continued insurance coverage, the amount added to the lump sum is explicitly defined as the present value of the projected cost for 18 months of coverage.

Under the federal golden parachute rules referenced in the recitals—12 U.S.C. 1828(k)(4)(A)(ii) and 12 C.F.R. 359.1(f)(1)(ii)—the agreement expressly makes all severance benefits contingent on prior FDIC approval if Middlefield later becomes troubled, notwithstanding section 1’s payment terms.

Under the combined operation of sections 1(a) and 1(c), if Middlefield elects to pay the present-value cash equivalent in lieu of continued insurance coverage, that additional cash amount is itself subject to the two-times multiplier applied to annual compensation, effectively tripling the Executive's potential lump-sum severance.

Under the definition of 'Change in Control' in section 2(b), the acquisition of 25% or more of Middlefield's voting securities by any person, including a subsidiary holding shares in a fiduciary capacity, triggers a Change in Control.

Section 1(c) effectively guarantees the Executive a full 24 months of continued life, health, and disability insurance coverage after a qualifying termination, even if the Executive becomes employed by another employer during that period.

Section 1(a) provides that the lump-sum severance payment equal to two times the Executive's annual compensation is payable no later than five business days after the date of employment termination, and this payment shall not be reduced, discounted to present value, or subject to any withholding or offset for amounts owed by the Executive to Middlefield.

Section 1(c) states that if Middlefield elects to pay a cash equivalent for continued life, health, and disability coverage instead of providing the coverage, the total lump-sum payment under Section 1(a) must be increased by the present value of the cost for 36 months of coverage.
